name: Dock Foreman
description: A stocky man with a clipboard, barking orders at dock workers between bites of a sandwich.

start.text: Can't talk long — shipment coming in. What d'you need?
start.narrative: The foreman checks his clipboard without looking up.

cargo.text: Grain, timber, fish, wool — the usual. Sometimes special orders from the jeweler or the blacksmith. And some crates I'm told not to ask about. That's above my pay grade.
cargo.narrative: He shrugs and takes another bite of his sandwich.

mystery.text: Look, I just move boxes. Harbour master handles the manifests. Some come in unmarked from the cove side. I load 'em, I don't look inside. Healthier that way.
mystery.narrative: He lowers his voice and glances around.
